bottleneck_classification.resolution_method: wall-clock stage timers as primary, cProfile cumulative hierarchy as secondary

candidate_classes_assessed.code_boundaries: frame_features.extract_features — compute_histogram (48.8s cumulative), cv2_to_gray (34.0s cumulative), phash, dhash
candidate_classes_assessed.ruling: PRIMARY — largest measured wall-clock stage; subsystem compute_histogram+cv2_to_gray accounts for 82.8s (78%) of this class

NOT_PRIMARY — cProfile cumulative is inflated; Packet.decode overlaps with extract_features in producer-consumer pipeline. Decode produces 18018 frames in 265s ≈ 68 fps, not a bottleneck at current HW (RTX 4090 + CUDA decode).
NOT_PRIMARY — only 4.1% of wall-clock; not worth optimizing before FEATURE_EXTRACTION_CPU
NOT_PRIMARY — visual_distance ~12.4s (4.7%); threshold and debounce are negligible (<1s)
NOT_SELECTED — wall-clock evidence clearly isolates FEATURE_EXTRACTION_CPU

optimization_selected.name: opencv-accelerated-histogram-gray
optimization_selected.optimization_hypothesis: Replace per-frame custom histogram computation with cv2.calcHist vectorized path; fuse grayscale conversion and histogram into a single pre-processing pass. Estimated wall-clock reduction: 30-50% of compute_histogram + cv2_to_gray subsystem (25-41s), translating to 9-15% of total pipeline.

acceptance_gates_for_step_18_6.benchmark_integrity: immutable short video, same HW (RTX 4090), same measurement protocol
